page: Amazon Web Services c6gn.medium is a compute-optimized instance powered by a dedicated AWS Graviton processor. It features 1 physical core and 1 vCPU based on the 64-bit ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz. The processor includes 64 KB of L1 data cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. This instance is equipped with 2.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, and runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators.

For networking, the instance provides a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps. In benchmark evaluations, the single-core performance of the AWS Graviton processor is moderate, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1492.69, placing it in the middle 50% tier. However, due to its single-vCPU configuration, multi-core benchmarks like stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core align exactly with its single-core metrics, resulting in lower relative multi-core performance compared to larger instances. Memory bandwidth is measured at 19.36 GB/sec for large-block reads and 9.74 GB/sec for large-block writes.

The c6gn.medium offers a highly cost-efficient entry point for ARM64-based development and testing. Given its hardware profile, it is suited for lightweight single-threaded applications, basic web serving, Redis caching, and minor utility scripts. It is not designed for memory-intensive databases, multi-threaded high-performance computing, or large language model inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c6gn.medium is an entry-level, compute-optimized instance featuring a single dedicated AWS Graviton core. Built on the 64-bit ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 architecture running at 2.5 GHz, it is equipped with 2.0 GB of system memory and 32 MB of L3 cache. The instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps, with no local storage or GPU acceleration. Benchmark data shows moderate single-core performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1492.69, though multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics reflect its single-vCPU constraints. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for lightweight, single-threaded workloads, including basic web serving, Redis caching, and ARM64 software development or testing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6gn.medium is a compute-optimized ARM64 instance featuring a single 2.5 GHz AWS Graviton core, 2.0 GB of RAM, and 2 Gbps network bandwidth, ideal for lightweight single-threaded workloads.

tagline: An entry-level, compute-optimized ARM64 instance featuring a dedicated AWS Graviton core and balanced network performance for lightweight workloads.
bullet_points: Single dedicated AWS Graviton ARM64 core running at 2.5 GHz
2.0 GB of system memory with a compute-optimized 1:2 resource ratio
Baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps on the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Moderate single-core performance suitable for lightweight utility tasks
Best suited for basic web serving, Redis caching, and ARM64 development
